variant: build me a full skincare starter guide: a product table, a timeline, a checklist, and key stats

request.message: build me a full skincare starter guide: a product table, a timeline, a checklist, and key stats

response_body.response:
you're 16 with combination skin and no major concerns, so the goal is prevention + maintenance. build a solid foundation now and you'll skip years of damage repair later.

**the core system**: cleanser → active (one) → hydrating serum → moisturizer → spf (am only). that's it. one active at a time, no stacking. your skin will thank you.

products.name: CeraVe Hydrating Facial Cleanser
products.description: Ceramide-based, fragrance-free, non-stripping — pairs with any active.

products.name: The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%
products.description: 10% niacinamide for sebum control and pore appearance.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

steps.label: week 1–2
steps.detail: establish routine. skin may feel tight or slightly dry as barrier adjusts. this is normal.

steps.label: week 3–4
steps.detail: barrier strengthens. hydration improves, skin feels smoother. introduce active (retinoid or niacinamide) 1–2x if not already.

steps.label: week 5–8
steps.detail: texture refines. pores appear smaller, tone evens slightly. consistency matters—don't skip.

steps.label: week 9–12
steps.detail: visible clarity and glow. skin is more resilient. you'll notice uv protection working if you've been consistent with spf.

items: get a gentle cleanser (cerave hydrating or similar)
pick one active: retinoid (start low, 0.025%) or niacinamide 5% serum
buy a hydrating serum with hyaluronic acid
choose a moisturizer with ceramides (cerave, la roche-posay)
get spf 30+ for daily am use—non-negotiable
set am + pm reminders in your planner (consistency > perfection)
test patch the active on your jaw first (48h before full face)
commit to 8+ weeks before judging results
